name: fastdevcli-slim

metadata.name: fastdevcli-slim
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules

documentation:
Toolkit for python code lint/test/bump ...

--- **Source Code**: https://github.com/waketzheng/fast-dev-cli **English** | [中文](./README.zh.md) ## Requirements Python 3.9+ ## Installation
```bash pip install fast-dev-cli ``` *Will install: fast-dev-cli emoji typer ruff mypy bumpversion2 pytest coverage*
## Usage - Lint py code: ```bash fast lint [/path/to/file-or-directory] ``` - Check only ```bash fast check ``` - Bump up version in pyproject.toml ```bash fast bump patch # 0.1.0 -> 0.1.1 fast bump minor # 0.1.0 -> 0.2.0 fast bump major # 0.1.0 -> 1.0.0 fast bump --commit # bump version and run `git commit` ``` - Run unittest and report coverage ```bash fast test ``` - Export requirement file and install `pip install -r ` ```bash fast sync ``` - Upgrade main/dev dependencies to latest version (only for poetry project) ```bash fast upgrade ``` - Start a fastapi server in development mode ```bash fast dev ``` - Publish to pypi ```bash fast upload ``` *Note: all command support the `--dry` option*

functions.name: tests.utils.capture_stdout
documentation:
Redirect sys.stdout to a new StringIO

Example::
py

> with capture_stdout() as stream:
>
> : GitTag(message="", dry=True).run()
>
> assert "git tag -a" in stream.getvalue()

classes.name: tests.utils.chdir
documentation: Non thread-safe context manager to change the current working directory.

methods.name: fast_dev_cli.cli.BumpUp.should_add_emoji
documentation:
If last commit message is startswith emoji,
add a ⬆️ flag at the prefix of bump up commit message.

classes.name: fast_dev_cli.cli.EnvError
documentation: Raise when expected to be managed by poetry, but toml file not found.

classes.name: fast_dev_cli.cli.ParseError
documentation: Raise this if parse dependence line error

methods.name: fast_dev_cli.cli.UpgradeDependencies.parse_value
documentation:
Pick out the value for key in version info.

Example::
: ```pycon
  >>> s= 'typer = {extras = ["all"], version = "^0.9.0", optional = true}'
  >>> UpgradeDependencies.parse_value(s, 'extras')
  'all'
  >>> UpgradeDependencies.parse_value(s, 'optional')
  'true'
  >>> UpgradeDependencies.parse_value(s, 'version')
  '^0.9.0'
  ```
